fileDoc.summary: Built-in types, functions, and members available in every Azora program without imports.
fileDoc.description: Primitive types: Int, UInt, Long, ULong, Byte, UByte, Short, UShort, Cent, UCent, Float, Real, Decimal, Bool, Char, String, Unit. Control flow: if/else, while, for-in-range, loop, break, continue. Enums and when/match. Tuples. Error handling: throw, try/catch, expr catch fallback. Other built-ins: var/let/fin bindings, functions, pack structs, string interpolation, zone/friend-zone/scoping, inline/deepinline compile-time execution, test/assert/trace. A full standard library (containers, math, algorithms, concurrency) is designed but not yet implemented.

doc.summary: Prints a single value followed by a newline.
doc.description: Accepts any type: Int, Real, Bool, String, Char, arrays, and structs.

doc.summary: The number of elements in an array, or characters in a string.
True if the array has no elements.
True if the array has at least one element.
Appends an element to the end of an array, growing it by one.
Raises a value (of any type) as a throwable.
doc.description: Caught by try/catch or the catch expression.

doc.summary: Matches a value against pattern branches.
doc.description: The first branch whose patterns (comma-separated) equal the value runs. An optional else branch handles the rest.

fileDoc.description: Provides higher-level concurrency patterns built on top of the language's coroutine primitives: timeout, retry, and parallel execution.

declarations.name: timeout
declarations.signature: task timeout<T>(ms: T, action: () -> Unit): Bool

doc.summary: Runs an action with a timeout.
doc.description: Executes the action and checks if it completed within the specified duration. Uses cooperative scheduling via suspend.

tags.return: true if the action completed, false otherwise.

declarations.name: handle
declarations.signature: fin handle = async { action(); completed = true }

declarations.name: retry
declarations.signature: task retry<T>(attempts: T, delayMs: T, action: () -> Bool): Bool

doc.summary: Retries an action up to the specified number of attempts.
doc.description: After each failed attempt, waits for the specified delay before retrying.

param.name: delayMs
param.description: The delay between attempts in milliseconds.

param.name: action
param.description: The function to execute. Should return true on success.

tags.return: true if any attempt succeeded, false if all attempts failed.

declarations.name: parallel
declarations.signature: task parallel<T>(tag: T, a: () -> Unit, b: () -> Unit)

doc.summary: Runs two actions concurrently and waits for both to complete.

declarations.name: race
declarations.signature: task race<T>(tag: T, a: () -> Unit, b: () -> Unit)

doc.summary: Runs two actions concurrently, completing when the first finishes.

declarations.name: t1
declarations.signature: fin t1 = async { a(); done = true }

declarations.name: t2
declarations.signature: fin t2 = async { b(); done = true }

fileDoc.description: Provides a FIFO message queue for communication between threads, with send, receive, and close operations protected by a mutex.

doc.summary: A thread-safe FIFO message channel for inter-thread communication.
doc.description: Channel provides a synchronized queue that allows threads to send and receive messages safely.

param.name: T
param.description: The type of messages in this channel.

doc.summary: The number of messages currently in the channel.
Whether the channel has no messages.
Whether the channel has been closed.
Sends a message to the channel.

throws: AssertionError if the channel is closed.

doc.summary: Receives and removes the next message from the channel.

tags.return: The next message.
throws: AssertionError if the channel is empty.

doc.summary: Attempts to receive a message without blocking.

param.name: default
param.description: The value to return if the channel is empty.

tags.return: The next message, or the default value if empty.

doc.summary: Closes the channel, preventing further sends.

fileDoc.description: Provides functions for converting primitive types to strings and string formatting operations like padding, repeating, and joining.

doc.summary: Converts an integer to its string representation.

tags.return: The string representation.

doc.summary: Converts a real number to its string representation.

tags.return: The string representation.

doc.summary: Converts a boolean to its string representation.

tags.return: "true" or "false".

declarations.signature: func padRight<T>(s: T, count: Int, char: T): T

param.name: count
param.description: The number of padding characters to add.

tags.return: The padded string.

fileDoc.description: Defines fundamental specifications for type capabilities: equality comparison, ordering, and string representation.

doc.summary: Types that support equality comparison via == and !=.
Int supports equality comparison.
Real supports equality comparison.
String supports equality comparison.
Bool supports equality comparison.
Types that support ordering via < and >.
Int supports ordering.
Real supports ordering.
String supports ordering.
Types that have a string representation.
Int has a string representation.
Real has a string representation.
String has a string representation.
Bool has a string representation.

fileDoc.description: Provides task-based utilities for asynchronous execution, including delayed actions.

doc.summary: Executes an action after a specified delay.
doc.description: Suspends for the given number of milliseconds, then invokes the action.

param.name: ms
param.description: The delay in milliseconds before executing the action.

param.name: action
param.description: The function to execute after the delay.

fileDoc.description: Provides additional math utilities including exponentiation, factorial, GCD/LCM, parity checks, and array aggregation.

tags.return: n! (n factorial).

fileDoc.description: Provides a dynamic array-backed list with support for element access, modification, search, functional operations, and slicing. Uses pointer-based storage with explicit capacity management.

Checks whether the list contains the given element.

fileDoc.description: Provides an associative array backed by parallel pointer arrays, with support for insertion, lookup, removal, and functional iteration. Uses pointer-based storage with explicit capacity management.

doc.summary: A generic key-value dictionary using parallel pointer arrays.

Associates the given key with the given value.
doc.description: If the key already exists, its value is replaced.

param.description: The key to insert or update.

param.name: value
param.description: The value to associate with the key.

doc.summary: Returns the value associated with the given key.

tags.return: The associated value.
throws: AssertionError if the key is not found.

tags.return: A string in the format "{key1: value1, key2: value2}".

fileDoc.summary: Core mathematical constants and utility functions.
fileDoc.description: Provides fundamental constants (PI, E, TAU) and common numeric operations such as absolute value, clamping, and linear interpolation.

doc.summary: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.
Euler's number, the base of natural logarithms.
The ratio of a circle's circumference to its radius (2 * PI).

declarations.name: abs
declarations.signature: func abs<T>(x: T): T = if x < 0

doc.summary: Returns the absolute value of the given number.

param.name: x
param.description: The value to take the absolute value of.

tags.return: The non-negative magnitude of x.

declarations.name: min
declarations.signature: func min<T>(a: T, b: T): T = if a < b

doc.summary: Returns the smaller of two values.

tags.return: The lesser of a and b.

declarations.name: max
declarations.signature: func max<T>(a: T, b: T): T = if a > b

doc.summary: Returns the larger of two values.

tags.return: The greater of a and b.

declarations.name: clamp
declarations.signature: func clamp<T>(x: T, lo: T, hi: T): T = max<T>(lo, min<T>(x, hi))

doc.summary: Constrains a value within the given range.

param.name: lo
param.description: The lower bound of the range.

param.name: hi
param.description: The upper bound of the range.

tags.return: x if it falls within [lo, hi], otherwise the nearest bound.

declarations.name: sign
declarations.signature: func sign<T>(x: T): Int = if x > 0

doc.summary: Returns the sign of a number as an integer.

param.name: x
param.description: The value to determine the sign of.

tags.return: 1 if x is positive, -1 if negative, 0 if zero.

declarations.name: lerp
declarations.signature: func lerp(a: Real, b: Real, t: Real): Real = a + (b - a) * t

doc.summary: Performs linear interpolation between two values.

tags.return: The interpolated value a + (b - a) * t.

fileDoc.description: Provides a first-in-first-out queue backed by a pointer array, with enqueue, dequeue, and peek operations. Uses pointer-based storage with explicit capacity management.

doc.summary: A generic first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue.
doc.description: Queue provides pointer-backed storage where elements are added at the back and removed from the front.

throws: AssertionError if the queue is empty.

tags.return: The front element.
throws: AssertionError if the queue is empty.

doc.summary: Removes all elements from the queue.
Returns a string representation of the queue.

tags.return: A string in the format "[front, ..., back]".

fileDoc.description: Provides linear search, binary search, min/max finding, and occurrence counting for arrays.

declarations.name: binarySearch
declarations.signature: func binarySearch<T>(arr: T[], target: T): Int

doc.summary: Performs a binary search on a sorted array.
doc.description: The array must be sorted in ascending order for correct results.

tags.return: The index of the target, or -1 if not found.

doc.summary: Returns the minimum element in an array.

param.name: arr
param.description: The array to search (must be non-empty).

tags.return: The smallest element.

doc.summary: Returns the maximum element in an array.

param.name: arr
param.description: The array to search (must be non-empty).

tags.return: The largest element.

declarations.name: count
declarations.signature: func count<T>(arr: T[], target: T): Int

doc.summary: Counts the occurrences of a target in an array.

fileDoc.description: Provides a pointer-backed set that maintains uniqueness of elements, with support for standard set operations like union, intersection, and difference. Uses pointer-based storage with explicit capacity management.

tags.return: A string in the format "{element1, element2, ...}".

fileDoc.description: Provides stable insertion sort, descending sort, sorted check, and array reversal operations.

doc.summary: Sorts an array in ascending order using insertion sort.
doc.description: Returns a new sorted array; the original is not modified.

tags.return: A new array with elements in ascending order.

doc.summary: Sorts an array in descending order.

tags.return: A new array with elements in descending order.

fileDoc.description: Provides a last-in-first-out stack backed by a pointer array, with push, pop, and peek operations. Uses pointer-based storage with explicit capacity management.

doc.summary: A generic last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack.
doc.description: Stack provides pointer-backed storage where elements are added and removed from the top.

param.name: T
param.description: The type of elements stored in this stack.

doc.summary: The number of elements in the stack.
Whether the stack contains no elements.
Whether the stack contains at least one element.
Pushes an element onto the top of the stack.
Removes and returns the top element of the stack.

tags.return: The removed top element.
throws: AssertionError if the stack is empty.

doc.summary: Returns the top element without removing it.

tags.return: The top element.
throws: AssertionError if the stack is empty.

doc.summary: Removes all elements from the stack.
Returns a string representation of the stack.

tags.return: A string in the format "[bottom, ..., top]".

fileDoc.description: Provides higher-level synchronization utilities built on top of the language's atomic and mutex primitives: Guard for safe shared state, CountDownLatch for coordination, and SpinLock for lightweight locking.

doc.summary: A mutex-protected value that provides safe concurrent access.
doc.description: Guard wraps a value with a mutex, ensuring that reads and writes are always performed under lock protection.

param.name: T
param.description: The type of the protected value.

doc.summary: Reads the inner value under lock.

param.name: action
param.description: The function to execute with the current value.

doc.summary: Modifies the inner value under lock.

param.name: action
param.description: The function that receives the current value and returns the new value.

doc.summary: Returns a snapshot of the current value.

tags.return: The current value.

doc.summary: A coordination primitive that allows threads to wait until

param.name: T
param.description: The type of the count value.

doc.summary: Decrements the latch count by one.
Whether the latch count has been reached.
A lightweight CAS-based spin lock.
doc.description: SpinLock uses an atomic flag for minimal-overhead mutual exclusion. Best suited for very short critical sections.

param.name: T
param.description: Unused type parameter for template registration.

doc.summary: Acquires the lock, spinning until successful.
Releases the lock.
Executes an action while holding the lock.

param.name: action
param.description: The function to execute under lock.

doc.summary: Executes an action under the given mutex lock.

param.name: action
param.description: The function to execute while holding the lock.

fileDoc.description: Provides basic threading primitives including sleep, spawn, and thread joining.

doc.summary: Suspends the current task for the given number of milliseconds.
Spawns a new concurrent task that runs the given action.

param.name: ms
param.description: A scheduling hint in milliseconds (pass 0 for immediate).

doc.summary: Suspends the current task for the given number of milliseconds.

fileDoc.summary: Core type traits and promotion system.
fileDoc.description: Defines the Numeric spec for arithmetic types and the compile-time type promotion function for mixed-type expressions.

doc.summary: A specification that marks types supporting arithmetic operations.
doc.description: Types implementing Numeric can be used with standard math operators and the promote type function.

doc.summary: Int satisfies the Numeric spec.
Real satisfies the Numeric spec.
Determines the widest numeric type from a set of types at compile time.
doc.description: Given two or more types, returns the type with the highest rank, enabling safe implicit promotion in mixed-type arithmetic.

param.name: T
param.description: Variadic type parameter pack of types to compare.

tags.return: The type with the highest numeric rank.

fileDoc.description: A Tuple holds two or more values of potentially different types, accessible by their positional index.

doc.summary: A fixed-size, ordered collection of heterogeneous values.
doc.description: Each element is accessible by its zero-based positional index. Tuples require at least two elements.

param.name: T
param.description: Variadic type parameter pack for the element types.
